last_result.summary: Discovery completed for the M0 factory wave. User decisions: MIT license; one pinned Linux x86-64 reference environment with no broader M0 compatibility claim; outside contributions closed during M0; GitHub Actions for functional, sanitizer, and advisory benchmark checks, with authoritative performance gates restricted to controlled-runner measurements. All foundational seed assumptions A-D04 through A-D08 are confirmed. No critical unknowns or deferred questions remain.

Completed the self-contained Orus foundation specification pack for the M0 factory wave. The charter defines measurable M0 scope and the complete M0-M11 milestone roadmap; the glossary standardizes product and engineering terminology; the decision log maps every discovery answer and binding architectural tradeoff with alternatives and validation; the risk register provides concrete mitigations and milestone validation; and SPECS.md indexes all foundation files plus 28 dependency-ordered domain-spec placeholders with the required reusable template. Discovery is closed and no blocking ambiguity remains.

First-round foundation verification found 10 blocking findings: three Charter testability gaps, four Glossary completeness/consistency defects, two Risk traceability defects, and premature Ready declarations in SPECS.md. The ADR log is READY, all required foundation files exist, and no planned specs/10+ file was treated as missing.

open_questions.text: What exact copyright year and legal holder name should appear in the root MIT LICENSE and packaged copies?
open_questions.why_it_matters: The Charter requires the standard MIT text and packaged license validation, but the required notice value cannot be derived from discovery, the decisions, or the project seed.
open_questions.unlocks: Final root LICENSE content, packaged-license checks, and release evidence for G-03, SM-10, and DOD-06.
open_questions.default: Require an owner-supplied notice in the release domain specification and block public packaging; do not invent a holder or ship a placeholder.
